name: Redeemer
description_short: Redeemer FCU is a federal credit union offering checking, savings, vehicle loans, and personal loans to its members with competitive rates and low fees.
description_long:
Redeemer FCU is a federally chartered credit union that serves as a member-owned financial institution. Based on the website content, it appears to be affiliated with or serves Halliburton employees, though membership details are not fully specified beyond the $10 minimum deposit requirement for a regular share savings account. The credit union operates with a focus on providing personalized member service and straightforward financial products.

Redeemer FCU offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including regular checking accounts, savings accounts (Regular Shares), and multiple savings vehicles like Share Certificates (CDs) with 6, 12, and 24-month terms. Members can access specialized savings products including Christmas Club accounts (up to $5,000 limit) and Vacation Club accounts. On the lending side, they provide vehicle loans for new and used cars (APR from 5.49%), personal loans (APR from 11.74%), and share-secured or unsecured loans (APR as low as Rate + 3%). Additional services include direct deposit, money orders, cashier's checks, and online/mobile banking with bill pay functionality.

As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, Redeemer FCU distinguishes itself through member ownership, dividend declarations by the Board of Directors, and a membership-based model. The organization emphasizes low fees and personalized service. Payroll deduction options are available for savings products, and funds can be transferred to regular share accounts on specified dates (May 1st for most products, October 1st for Christmas Club). The routing number provided is 111994296.

The main limitation is that membership eligibility is not clearly defined on the public-facing website, suggesting restricted membership criteria. The company does not appear to offer credit cards, mortgages, business loans, or wealth management services. Website navigation indicates links to third-party sites and references to "Halliburton Employees' Federal Credit Union," which may indicate this is an employee credit union with limited public accessibility.
services: Christmas Club savings accounts (up to $5,000 limit)
Vehicle loans (new and used, APR from 5.49%)
Share-secured loans (APR as low as Rate + 3%)
Online and mobile banking with bill pay
pros: Auto loan APR starts at 5.49%, competitive for vehicle financing
Share Certificate (CD) terms available at 6, 12, and 24 months with board-declared competitive rates
Christmas Club and Vacation Club specialized savings accounts with automatic fund transfers
Payroll deduction available for savings products, enabling automatic contributions
Share-secured loans available at Rate + 3% for qualified members
Online and mobile banking with bill pay and direct deposit functionality
Low membership barrier with $10 minimum deposit to regular share account
Member-owned structure with Board-declared dividends rather than corporate profits
cons: Membership eligibility criteria not clearly defined on website; appears to be employee-restricted credit union
Personal loan APR of 11.74% is higher than some alternatives, limiting affordability for credit-challenged borrowers
No credit card products offered, limiting rewards or balance transfer options
Limited loan product range; no mortgages, business loans, or lines of credit
Website contains confusing dual branding references to 'Halliburton Employees' Federal Credit Union,' creating clarity issues
best_for: Eligible employees seeking low-cost vehicle financing and straightforward checking/savings accounts
Members wanting to save systematically for holidays or vacations with automatic payroll deduction
Credit union members preferring member-owned institutions over corporate banks
Individuals seeking competitive CD rates and share-secured lending options
diagnosis: Redeemer FCU is best for eligible members (likely Halliburton employees or affiliates) seeking low-cost vehicle financing, savings products, and basic banking services through a member-owned credit union. The primary caveat is that membership eligibility is restricted and not fully transparent on the public website, making accessibility dependent on employment or other membership criteria not disclosed.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
meta_title: Redeemer — Credit Union in Plano, TX | CreditDoc
meta_description: Redeemer — NCUA-insured credit union in Plano, TX. Member-owned, not-for-profit. Loans, savings, and member services.
